name: documentation-generation-doc-generate
description: You are a documentation expert specializing in creating comprehensive, maintainable documentation from code. Generate API docs, architecture diagrams, user guides, and technical references using AI-powered analysis and industry best practices.
capabilities: Extract API endpoints and parameters from source code to generate OpenAPI specifications
Create architecture diagrams using Mermaid or PlantUML from code structure analysis
Generate README files with setup instructions, configuration details, and usage examples
Produce user guides and tutorials with step-by-step workflows
Set up documentation CI/CD pipelines for automatic regeneration on code changes
Validate documentation coverage and identify undocumented functions or classes
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill contains documentation templates and example code snippets in markdown format. All detected patterns (shell commands, network calls, crypto references) exist within markdown code blocks as illustrative examples, not executable code. No actual security risks identified.
valueStatement: Keep your documentation synchronized with code automatically. This skill extracts information from source code to create API specs, architecture diagrams, and user guides that stay current as your codebase evolves.
limitations: Requires access to source code files for analysis
Generated documentation should be reviewed for accuracy before publishing
May not fully understand highly dynamic or metaprogramming patterns
Does not replace human technical writers for complex narrative documentation

useCases.targetUser: Backend developers maintaining REST or GraphQL APIs
useCases.title: API Documentation Generation
useCases.description: Automatically generate OpenAPI specifications and interactive API documentation from your codebase annotations and route definitions.

useCases.targetUser: Software architects documenting system design for teams
useCases.title: Architecture Documentation
useCases.description: Create system architecture diagrams and component documentation by analyzing imports, dependencies, and code structure.

useCases.title: Documentation Pipeline Automation
useCases.description: Set up CI/CD workflows that regenerate and deploy documentation on every code commit to keep docs synchronized.

promptTemplates.title: Basic API Documentation
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze the Python files in ./src/api/ directory and generate OpenAPI 3.0 specification with all endpoints, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements.

promptTemplates.title: README Generation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a comprehensive README.md for this repository including installation instructions, configuration options, usage examples, and contribution guidelines based on the code structure.

promptTemplates.title: Architecture Diagrams
promptTemplates.prompt: Examine the service modules and their imports to create Mermaid diagrams showing system architecture, data flow, and component dependencies.

promptTemplates.title: Full Documentation Suite
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate complete documentation including API reference, architecture overview, getting started guide, and deployment instructions. Set up a documentation site using the generated artifacts with search functionality.

bestPractices: Run documentation generation as part of CI/CD to keep docs synchronized with code changes
Include documentation coverage checks that fail builds when coverage drops below threshold
Review generated documentation for accuracy and add human context where needed
antiPatterns: Generating documentation once and never updating it as code evolves
Publishing generated documentation without human review for accuracy
Including sensitive information like API keys or internal URLs in generated docs

faq.question: What programming languages are supported for documentation generation?
faq.answer: The skill works best with Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, and Go. It can analyze other languages with AST-based documentation extraction but may have limited support for less common languages.

faq.question: Can this skill generate documentation for private repositories?
faq.answer: Yes, when running within Claude Code or Codex with access to your repository, the skill can analyze and generate documentation for private codebases.

faq.question: How do I keep generated documentation up to date?
faq.answer: Set up a CI/CD workflow that triggers documentation regeneration on code changes. The skill includes example GitHub Actions workflows for automated documentation pipelines.

faq.question: What documentation formats can be generated?
faq.answer: OpenAPI/Swagger specs, Markdown documentation, Mermaid diagrams, HTML documentation sites, and reStructuredText for Sphinx integration.

faq.question: Does this skill replace human technical writers?
faq.answer: No. It automates repetitive documentation tasks and generates reference documentation, but human writers are essential for narrative content, tutorials, and ensuring documentation quality.

faq.question: How accurate is the generated documentation?
faq.answer: Accuracy depends on code quality and annotations. Well-documented code with type hints and docstrings produces better results. Always review generated documentation before publishing.
